data.title: Vitalik: I am still very optimistic about the contribution and role of Argentina and Latin America in the crypto space
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News Ethereum co-founder Vitalik Buterin posted on the X platform that Argentina's Ethereum community is full of outstanding builders who have contributed some of the most important software to the Ethereum ecosystem. 
Argentina does have the determination and energy to continue the current momentum and use cryptocurrencies and other new technologies to build a prosperous 21st century society. Recent news should not be seen as a reason to give up, but as an example of why education is so important. 
Still very optimistic about Argentina and Latin America's contribution and role in this field.

data.title: Fed's Goolsbee: If tariffs lead to higher inflation, Fed will consider it
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Fed's Goolsbee: If tariffs lead to higher inflation, the Fed will consider this, and he said that PCE data is unlikely to be as alarming as CPI data. (Jinshi)

data.title: Federal Reserve Considers Tariff Impact on Inflation
data.description: According to Odaily, Federal Reserve official Goolsbee stated that if tariffs lead to increased inflation, the Federal Reserve will take this into consideration. He also mentioned that PCE data is unlikely to be as alarming as CPI data.

data.title: Cryptocurrency Market Experiences $145 Million Liquidation in 24 Hours
data.description: According to PANews, data from Coinglass reveals that the cryptocurrency market witnessed a total liquidation of $145 million in the past 24 hours. Long positions accounted for $53.99 million, while short positions saw liquidations amounting to $91.41 million. Bitcoin (BTC) experienced a total liquidation of $33.01 million, and Ethereum (ETH) faced liquidations totaling $18.35 million.

data.title: Coinbase considers listing KAITO
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News Coinbase posted on the X platform that it will add support for Kaito (KAITO) on the Base network. If liquidity conditions are met, trading will begin later today. Once sufficient asset supply is established, the KAITO-USD trading pair will be launched in stages. Support for KAITO may be limited in some supported jurisdictions.

data.title: Lending protocol PACT officially bridges to Aptos
Hyperfluid is now live on the public mainnet with LP Incentive
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Aptos ecosystem DEX Hyperfluid is now launched on the public mainnet through LP Incentive, which provides LP with an annual interest rate of 60%, seamless swap with CLMM, and a community-owned platform.

data.title: Federal Reserve's Goolsbee Comments On PCE Data Expectations
data.description: According to BlockBeats, on February 20, Federal Reserve official Austan Goolsbee stated that the Personal Consumption Expenditures (PCE) data is unlikely to be as concerning as the Consumer Price Index (CPI) data. This comment comes amid ongoing discussions about inflation and economic indicators in the United States.

data.title: The U.S. SEC approves blockchain finance company Figure to launch the first interest-bearing stablecoin
data.description:
The U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) has approved an application for a stablecoin with interest for the first time.
The stablecoin, called YLDS, was developed by digital asset company Figure Markets and will be pegged to the U.S. dollar and offer users a 0.5% yield, similar to cash in a savings account. While other major stablecoins such as USDC and USDT remain in a regulatory gray area, YLDS will be officially registered with the SEC as a security, which Figure CEO Mike Cagney said in an interview with Fortune, putting YLDS in the same financial category as stocks or bonds.
Documents on the SEC website show that Figure's application was approved on Tuesday. A spokesperson for the agency did not immediately respond to a request for comment. (fortune)

data.title: SEC Approves Interest-Bearing Stablecoin YLDS
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) has approved an application for an interest-bearing stablecoin, as reported by Fortune magazine on February 20. The stablecoin, named YLDS, is developed by digital asset company Figure Markets and is pegged to the U.S. dollar, offering users a 0.5% yield similar to cash in a savings account.

While major stablecoins like USDC and USDT remain in a regulatory gray area, YLDS is officially registered as a security with the SEC. Figure's CEO, Mike Cagney, stated in an interview with Fortune that this classification places YLDS in the same financial category as stocks or bonds.

Documents on the SEC's website indicate that Figure's application was approved on Tuesday. A spokesperson for the agency did not immediately respond to requests for comment.
